pages.title: Structure Rule Basics
pages.text: Structure rules are the $(bold)highest priority$() tier. When a mob spawns inside a structure with a rule, that rule wins.$(br2)File location:$(br2)$odata/<namespace>/mob_levels/structures/<name>.json$

pages.title: Required Fields
pages.text: $(bold)structure$() (string) - The structure ID:$(br2)$(li)Examples: $(o)minecraft:stronghold, minecraft:fortress, minecraft:end_city, minecraft:mansion$()$(br2)$(bold)level_range$() (object) - Min/max levels:$(br2){$(br)  "min": 50,$(br)  "max": 100$(br)}

pages.title: Optional Fields
pages.text: $(bold)priority$() (int, default: 100)$(br2)$(bold)enabled$() (boolean, default: true)$(br2)$(bold)level_mode$() (string: distance/random/fixed, default: random)$(br2)$(bold)fixed_level$() (int, required if level_mode=fixed)$(br2)$(bold)ignore_distance_scaling$() (boolean, default: true for structures)$(br2)$(bold)distance_multiplier$() (double, default: 1.0)

pages.title: Complex Fields
pages.text: $(bold)attribute_scaling$() - Map of attribute IDs to scaling configs$(br2)$(bold)mob_overrides$() - Per-mob exceptions with level_bonus, attribute_multipliers, etc:$(br2){$(br)  "minecraft:zombie": {$(br)    "fixed_level": 200,$(br)    "ignore_level_cap": true$(br)  }$(br)}$(br2)Structure rules with mob overrides can bypass passive/boss filters.
